subject: Geography
read_aloud: How many continents are there in the world?
correct_response: Yes! There are 7 continents on Earth!
incorrect_response: Not quite. There are more! Think of Asia, Africa, Europe, North America, South America, Antarctica!
hint: The seven continents are: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania (or Australia), South America!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the largest continent by area?
correct_response: Yes! Asia is the largest continent by area!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Africa is large too!
hint: Asia includes China, India, Japan and many other countries!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: Which continent is home to the United States?
correct_response: Yes! North America is where the USA is located!
incorrect_response: Not quite. USA is in North America!
hint: The USA has 50 states and Washington D.C. is the capital!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What continent is home to Brazil?
correct_response: Yes! South America is where Brazil is!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Brazil is in South America!
hint: Brazil has the Amazon Rainforest and the Christ the Redeemer statue!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: Which continent has the most countries?
correct_response: Yes! Europe has the most countries!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Asia has more people!
hint: Europe has countries like France, Germany, Italy, Spain and the UK!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: Which continent has Egypt and the pyramids?
correct_response: Yes! Egypt and the pyramids are in Africa!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The pyramids are in Egypt which is in Africa!
hint: Egypt is famous for its ancient pyramids and the Nile River!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: Which continent is home to koalas?
correct_response: Yes! Oceania is where koalas lives!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Koalas is in Australia!
hint: Koalas has kangaroos, koalas, and the Great Barrier Reef!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: Which continent has the Andes Mountains?
correct_response: Yes! The Andes Mountains run through several countries!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The Andes are in South America!
hint: The Andes include Aconcagua which is the highest peak in the Americas!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: Which continent has the Sahara Desert?
correct_response: Yes! The Sahara Desert is in Africa!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Deserts can be very hot and dry!
hint: The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert. Camels live there!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we call a low area of land?
correct_response: Yes! A valley is a low area between mountains!
incorrect_response: Not quite. A valley can have a river flowing through it!
hint: Many famous cities are in valleys like Kathmandu and Rome!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we call a very high area of land?
correct_response: Yes! A plateau is very high and flat!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The Tibetan Plateau is in Asia!
hint: Plateaus are high areas where the air is thin and it's hard to breathe!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we call a large area of water?
correct_response: Yes! An ocean is a very large area of water!
incorrect_response: Not quite. There are 5 oceans: Atlantic, Pacific, Indian, Arctic, and Southern!
hint: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is it like at the equator?
correct_response: Yes! The equator is always hot and sunny!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Near the equator it's hot all year!
hint: Countries like Kenya and Ecuador are on the equator!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is it like at the poles?
correct_response: Yes! The poles are very cold and have ice!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Both the North and South Poles are covered in ice!
hint: Polar bears and penguins live at the poles!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we see in the sky during the day?
correct_response: Yes! We see the sun during the day!
incorrect_response: Not quite. We see stars at night!
hint: The sun gives us light and warmth. Don't look at it directly!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we see in the sky at night?
correct_response: Yes! We see the moon and stars at night!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The moon is only visible at night!
hint: The moon is bright and round. It orbits the Earth once a month!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What happens when water gets very cold?
correct_response: Yes! Water freezes and becomes ice!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Water just gets cold!
hint: Ice can form on lakes, rivers, and oceans. Skating is fun!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is rain?
correct_response: Yes! Rain is water falling from clouds!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Rain helps plants grow!
hint: Rain comes from clouds in the sky!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What do we see after a storm?
correct_response: Yes! Rainbows have 7 colors in order: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple!
incorrect_response: Not quite. We see rainbows sometimes!
hint: Rainbows need rain and sun at the same time!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of China?
correct_response: Yes! Beijing is the capital of China!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Shanghai is also very big!
hint: Beijing is famous for the Forbidden City and the Great Wall of China!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of the UK?
correct_response: Yes! London is the capital of the UK!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Manchester and Birmingham are also big!
hint: London has the famous Big Ben clock tower and red phone boxes!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Japan?
correct_response: Yes! Tokyo is the capital of Japan!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Osaka is also a major city!
hint: Tokyo has many people and the famous Mount Fuji nearby!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of France?
correct_response: Yes! Paris is the capital of France!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Marseille is also important!
hint: Paris has the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre Museum!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Germany?
correct_response: Yes! Berlin is the capital of Germany!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Munich and Frankfurt are also big!
hint: Berlin was divided during the Cold War. Now it's united again!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Italy?
correct_response: Yes! Rome is the capital of Italy!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Milan is also very important!
hint: Rome has the Colosseum and the famous Trevi Fountain!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Russia?
correct_response: Yes! Moscow is the capital of Russia!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Saint Petersburg is also important!
hint: Moscow has the famous Red Square and colorful onion domes!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of India?
correct_response: Yes! New Delhi is the capital of India!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Mumbai is also huge!
hint: New Delhi has the famous India Gate and the Lotus Temple!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Canada?
correct_response: Yes! Ottawa is the capital of Canada!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Toronto and Montreal are also big!
hint: Canada has maple leaves and the famous Niagara Falls!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Australia?
correct_response: Yes! Canberra is the capital of Australia!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Sydney and Melbourne are also big!
hint: Australia has kangaroos, koalas, and the Great Barrier Reef!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Brazil?
correct_response: Yes! Brasilia is the capital of Brazil!
incorrect_response: Not quite. São Paulo is also huge!
hint: Brazil has the Amazon Rainforest and the Christ the Redeemer statue in Rio!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Argentina?
correct_response: Yes! Buenos Aires is the capital of Argentina!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Córdoba is also important!
hint: Argentina has tango dancing and the pampas in the grasslands!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Egypt?
correct_response: Yes! Cairo is the capital of Egypt!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Alexandria is also an ancient city!
hint: Cairo has the famous Pyramids of Giza and the Sphinx!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of South Africa?
correct_response: Yes! Pretoria is one of the capitals of South Africa!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Cape Town and Johannesburg are also big!
hint: Pretoria has the famous Union Buildings and the Voortrek Monument!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Nigeria?
correct_response: Yes! Abuja is the capital of Nigeria!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Lagos is also very big!
hint: Nigeria is in West Africa and has the Niger River!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Kenya?
correct_response: Yes! Nairobi is the capital of Kenya!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Mombasa is also important!
hint: Kenya is known for its amazing wildlife safaris like the Maasai Mara!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Morocco?
correct_response: Yes! Rabat is the capital of Morocco!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Casablanca and Marrakech are also important!
hint: Morocco is famous for its medinas in the desert!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of South Korea?
correct_response: Yes! Seoul is the capital of South Korea!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Busan is also important!
hint: South Korea is famous for its technology and K-pop music!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Spain?
correct_response: Yes! Madrid is the capital of Spain!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Barcelona is also famous!
hint: Spain has the famous Prado Museum and beautiful beaches!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Turkey?
correct_response: Yes! Ankara is the capital of Turkey!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Istanbul is also very big!
hint: Turkey connects Europe and Asia. The Bosphorus Strait is there!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the capital of Saudi Arabia?
correct_response: Yes! Riyadh is the capital of Saudi Arabia!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Jeddah and Mecca are also important holy cities!
hint: Saudi Arabia is the largest country and has vast deserts!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What famous landmark is in Paris?
correct_response: Yes! The Eiffel Tower is in Paris!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The Arc de Triomphe is also famous!
hint: The Eiffel Tower was built for the 1889 World's Fair!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What famous landmark is in London?
correct_response: Yes! Big Ben is the famous clock tower in London!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The London Eye is also popular!
hint: Big Ben shows the time with four clock faces!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What famous landmark is in New York City?
correct_response: Yes! The Statue of Liberty is in New York!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The Empire State Building is also famous!
hint: The Statue of Liberty was a gift from France in 1886!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What famous landmark is in Sydney?
correct_response: Yes! The Sydney Opera House is famous in Australia!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The Sydney Harbour Bridge is also iconic!
hint: The Sydney Opera House looks like white sails on the harbor!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What famous landmark is in Tokyo?
correct_response: Yes! The Tokyo Tower is a famous tower!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Tokyo Skytree is also very tall!
hint: The Tokyo Tower was built in 1958 and is 634 meters high!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the longest river in the world?
correct_response: Yes! The Nile River is the longest river in the world!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The Amazon River is also very long!
hint: The Nile flows through Egypt and empties into the Mediterranean Sea!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the highest waterfall in the world?
correct_response: Yes! Angel Falls in Venezuela is the world's highest waterfall!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Victoria Falls is also very tall!
hint: Angel Falls drops almost 1 kilometer! That's taller than 3 football fields!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the largest ocean by area?
correct_response: Yes! The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean by area!
incorrect_response: Not quite. The Atlantic Ocean is also very large!
hint: The Pacific Ocean covers one third of Earth and has the Mariana Trench!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the smallest continent by area?
correct_response: Yes! Australia is the smallest continent!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Australia is still very big!
hint: Australia is both a continent and a country!

subject: Geography
read_aloud: What is the largest city in the world?
correct_response: Yes! Tokyo is the largest city in the world!
incorrect_response: Not quite. Shanghai has over 20 million people!
hint: Tokyo has the busiest trains and the famous Shibuya Crossing!
